even_plus_even.statement: The sum of any two even integers is an even integer.
even_plus_even.definitions: An integer x is even if x = 2a for some integer a.
even_plus_even.note: Classic textbook proof - almost certainly memorized. Use as a baseline.
even_plus_even.flawed_proof: Let x = 2a and y = 2b be even integers. Then x + y = 2a + 2b. Factoring out the 2 gives x + y = 2(a + b) + 1. Since there is a +1, the result is one more than a multiple of 2. ANSWER: The sum of two even integers is odd.
even_plus_even.bug: Invents a spurious +1 when factoring: 2a + 2b = 2(a + b), not 2(a + b) + 1. The correct conclusion is even, not odd.

even_plus_odd.statement: The sum of an even integer and an odd integer is always odd.
even_plus_odd.definitions: x is even if x = 2a; x is odd if x = 2a + 1, for some integer a.
even_plus_odd.note: Perturbed variant. Less commonly recited verbatim, so it leans more on reasoning.
even_plus_odd.flawed_proof: Let e = 2a be even and o = 2b + 1 be odd. Then e + o = 2a + 2b + 1. We can factor this as e + o = 2(a + b + 1). Since this is 2 times a whole number, it is even. ANSWER: The sum of an even and an odd integer is even.
even_plus_odd.bug: Wrongly factors 2a + 2b + 1 as 2(a + b + 1); that expands to 2a + 2b + 2, not 2a + 2b + 1. The trailing +1 cannot be absorbed into the factor of 2, so the result is odd.

product_of_odds.statement: The product of any two odd integers is odd.
product_of_odds.definitions: x is odd if x = 2a + 1 for some integer a.
product_of_odds.note: Requires expanding (2a+1)(2b+1) and refactoring - a real derivation step.
product_of_odds.flawed_proof: Let x = 2a + 1 and y = 2b + 1 be odd. Then x * y = (2a + 1)(2b + 1) = 4ab + 2a + 2b + 1. Group everything under a factor of 2: x * y = 2(2ab + a + b + 1). This is 2 times a whole number, so it is even. ANSWER: The product of two odd integers is even.
product_of_odds.bug: Absorbs the trailing +1 into the factor of 2: 2(2ab + a + b + 1) expands to 4ab + 2a + 2b + 2, which is one too many. Correctly it is 2(2ab + a + b) + 1, which is odd.

sqrt2_irrational.statement: The square root of 2 cannot be written as a fraction of two integers.
sqrt2_irrational.definitions: A number is rational if it equals a/b for integers a, b with b != 0 and the fraction in lowest terms.
sqrt2_irrational.note: Proof by contradiction. Memorized but multi-step; good stress test for the verify loop.

triangle_angle_sum.statement: The interior angles of any triangle add up to 180 degrees.
triangle_angle_sum.definitions: Alternate interior angles formed by a line crossing two parallel lines are equal. A straight line measures 180 degrees.
triangle_angle_sum.note: Geometric; harder for text-only models because it relies on a construction.

sum_first_n.statement: For every positive integer n, 1 + 2 + ... + n = n(n+1)/2.
sum_first_n.definitions: Proof by induction: prove it for n = 1, then assume it for n = k and prove it for n = k + 1.
sum_first_n.note: Induction. Genuinely tests whether the model can carry structure, not just recall.
